{"key_event":"CCP victory and PRC founding","reasoning":"Although most of the decade featured extreme fragmentation, KMT fiscal collapse, occupation, famine, and civil war, by 1949 the CCP had destroyed the main KMT armies and founded a centralized party-state with expanding coercive, fiscal, and administrative reach. Civil society retained moments of urban protest and peasant mobilization, but war devastation, hyperinflation, land reform violence, party rectification, and incorporation into CCP-controlled mass organizations sharply reduced autonomous capacity.","society_change":-1.6,"society_power":3.2,"state_change":2.1,"state_power":6.5}
